question: Are the upper garments of the two men actively playing chess (hands near the board) replaced with Tang-style jackets?
Do the new jackets feature traditional front-opening closures with knotted buttons (frog buttons)?
Do the jackets feature standing collars (Mandarin collars) consistent with Tang-style clothing?
Is exactly one of the new jackets dark navy in color?
Is exactly one of the new jackets grayish brown in color?
Do the new garments display realistic dappled sunlight and shadow patterns consistent with the rest of the scene?
Are the sleeves and cuffs of the jackets aligned naturally with the players' arms and wrists, handling occlusions by the table correctly?
Is the man on the far left (spectator holding a cup) still wearing his original grey polo shirt?
Is the man in the center (spectator facing forward) still wearing his original light grey shirt?
Is the man on the far right (spectator) still wearing his original light grey striped shirt?

Are the lower garments (pants) of the chess players unchanged?
Are the objects on the table (chessboard, pieces, tea pot, snacks) preserved without distortion or displacement?
